title: The latency/throughput tradeoff in batching
description: Larger batches raise throughput and GPU efficiency but add queue wait; the optimum is the largest batch whose tail stays under the SLO.
body: Use when reasoning about serving performance. Accelerators process a batch nearly as fast as a single request, so larger batches multiply throughput and utilization — but requests wait to fill the batch, raising latency, especially the tail. There is no free lunch: every throughput gain from bigger batches costs queueing latency. Do not maximize one in isolation. Actions: choose the batch/wait that meets p99 at target QPS. Validation signals include a utilization-vs-p99 curve showing the knee. Risk boundary: pushing batch size for throughput without watching the tail silently breaks the latency SLO.
